title: Getting Started with biblioview
text: biblioview package provides lightweight, performant Shiny interface display Zotero bibliographies real time. Designed embedding inside Learning Management Systems (LMS) like OPAL, project websites, intranets, allows research projects course instructors maintain single source truth Zotero without requiring every student collaborator hold Zotero account.

title: Getting Started with biblioview
text: stream literature live biblioview, need shared Zotero Group Library read-API Key. Log zotero.org go Groups →\rightarrowCreate New Group. Public, Closed Membership: Recommended courses (anyone link/app can view, can edit). Private: Strictly hidden web; requires account API key access. Note Group ID group’s URL (zotero.org/groups/<group_id>). Go Account Settings →\rightarrowFeeds/API →\rightarrowCreate new private key. Give descriptive name (e.g., biblioview-course). Check Allow library access target group. Copy generated key string immediately save secure place. Security Note: Treat API key like password. Avoid hardcoding active API keys publicly accessible repositories. production, pass credentials using environment variables application configuration files.

title: Getting Started with biblioview

Copy generated key string immediately save secure place. Security Note: Treat API key like password. Avoid hardcoding active API keys publicly accessible repositories. production, pass credentials using environment variables application configuration files.

title: Getting Started with biblioview
text: biblioview supports URL parameters (query strings) control literature subset fetched header rendered. allows single Shiny app deployment serve multiple courses, project modules, web pages dynamically. folder omitted, biblioview defaults fetching entire top-level group library. title omitted, defaults standard application header.

title: Getting Started with biblioview
text: folder omitted, biblioview defaults fetching entire top-level group library. title omitted, defaults standard application header.

title: Getting Started with biblioview
text: biblioview provides two distinct app interfaces depending deployment target. Designed research projects, complex students courses administrators need full exploration capabilities. Key Features: Full-text search across titles authors, expandable abstracts, direct DOI/URL links, user-defined Note:-field extras. Access data can entered interactively provided URL. Retrieves available sub-collections (folders) allows dynamic selection. Supports enrichment bib entries citation counts abstracts, retrieved Crossref OpenAlex.  Designed specifically <iframe> embedding web pages learning management (LMS) platforms like OPAL Moodle. Key Features: Compact vertical layout without menus, reduced number columns, local caching (5 minutes) reduce Zotero server load, support customized CSS-styles. sidebar menu, must always called URL parameters.
code: <!-- Example HTML Module Embed --> <iframe    src="[https://your-shiny-server.org/biblioview-embed/?group=1234567&key=YOUR_KEY&folder=A1B2C3D4&title=Required%20Readings](https://your-shiny-server.org/bibliotable/?group=1234567&key=YOUR_KEY&folder=A1B2C3D4&title=Required%20Readings)"    width="100%"    height="600px"    frameborder="0"> </iframe>

Retrieves available sub-collections (folders) allows dynamic selection. Supports enrichment bib entries citation counts abstracts, retrieved Crossref OpenAlex.

title: Getting Started with biblioview
text: local development package testing (R CMD check), mock credentials can used combination offline fallback datasets ensure vignette compilation without live network calls:
code: library(biblioview)  # Example: Launching the app locally with demo parameters if (interactive()) {   launch_dashboard(     group  = "0000000",     key    = "INVALID_DEMO_KEY",     folder = "DEMO123",     title  = "Demo Literature View"   ) }

what: A Shiny Web Portal and Abstract Enricher for Zotero Libraries
title: A Shiny Web Portal and Abstract Enricher for Zotero Libraries
text: biblioview R package interactive Shiny dashboard designed streamline, enrich, explore literature libraries directly Zotero Group Libraries. implements resource-polite, multi-pass pipeline pull base metadata, resolve missing abstracts, track live citation metrics.

title: A Shiny Web Portal and Abstract Enricher for Zotero Libraries
text: Selective Collection Mapping: Scan select specific non-hierarchical sub-folders within Zotero group library pulling data . Batch Citation Metrics: Mass-queries OpenAlex API unified chunks 50 items , populating --date citation counts. Enrichment: Automatically checks Crossref, OpenAlex Europe PMC via fallback loop patch missing abstract text. Sorting, Filtering Export: Retrieved libraries can sorted, filtered exported.

title: A Shiny Web Portal and Abstract Enricher for Zotero Libraries
text: Install development version biblioview directly GitHub:

title: A Shiny Web Portal and Abstract Enricher for Zotero Libraries
text: can start bundled Shiny dashboard directly package environment using launch_dashboard(). launcher function accepts optional arguments pre-populate credentials dynamically customize application window title startup, removing need manage complex URL query strings manually. Deployment Note: hosting dashboard server infrastructure (Shiny Server Posit Connect), credentials can passed dynamically via standard web URL routing parameter rules instead (e.g., https://-shiny-server.org/biblioview/?group=1234567&key=your_secret_zotero_key&title=Water%20Quality%20Research%20Portal).
code: library(biblioview)  # Option A: Launch a clean, empty dashboard launch_dashboard()  # Option B: Launch pre-configured for a project team launch_dashboard(   group = "1234567",   key   = "your_secret_zotero_key",   title = "Water Quality Research Portal" )

title: A Shiny Web Portal and Abstract Enricher for Zotero Libraries
text: building custom analysis pipelines, can skip UI run modular batch operations directly R scripts:
code: library(biblioview)  # 1. Grab metadata from a specific collection sub-folder raw_data <- fetch_all_zotero_data(   group_id = "numeric_group_id",    api_key = "your_secret_zotero_key",   collection_id = "collection_id" )  # 2. Pull live batch metrics from OpenAlex final_dataset <- fetch_citation_counts(abstract_enriched, email_contact)  # 3. Patch gaps in abstract listings abstract_enriched <- enrich_missing_abstracts(raw_data, email_contact)

title: A Shiny Web Portal and Abstract Enricher for Zotero Libraries
text: layout operates progressive workflow loop protect network resources keep UI performance snappy: Step 0 (Scan Folders): Authenticates credentials Zotero API dynamically generates multi-select dropdown map target sub-collections. Step 1 (Fetch Selected Library): Downloads reference items isolates row entries drop duplicate DOIs. Step 2 (Fetch Citation Metrics): Triggers fast-batch OpenAlex lookup append citation records. step leverages optional API email input text box, defaulting system’s POLITE_EMAIL variable left blank. Step 3 (Run Abstract Enrichment): Executes defensive, item--item fallback abstract search queries.

title: A Shiny Web Portal and Abstract Enricher for Zotero Libraries

title: Fetch Citation Counts from OpenAlex via Batch Queries — fetch_citation_counts
text: Scans data frame DOI column, extracts valid identifiers, queries OpenAlex API batches 50 items retrieve --date citation metrics (cited_by_count). results joined back original data frame new citations column.
